name: Path of Fuente de Ca l'Enric and Ermita de San Bernabé — circular from Òdena
name_original: Camino de Fuente de Ca l'Enric y Ermita de San Bernabé — circular desde Òdena
region_intro: Jogging around Òdena offers a variety of routes through a landscape characterized by rolling hills, agricultural fields, and scattered woodlands. The region features moderate elevation changes, providing varied terrain for runners. Paths often lead through rural areas, connecting small villages and historical sites. This environment supports both shorter, easier jogging routes and longer, more challenging running trails. Best jogging routes around Òdena The most popular jogging route is Path of Fuente de Ca l'Enric and Ermita de San Bernabé — circular from Òdena, a 10.9 miles (17.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 2 minutes to complete. This difficult circular route passes by historical landmarks and natural springs. Another top favourite among local runners is Castell d'Òdena loop from Òdena, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.1 km) path. This route offers views of the historic Castell d'Òdena and the surrounding countryside. Local runners also love the Running loop from Igualada, a 5.5 miles (8.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain near the town of Igualada, often completed in about 57 minutes. Jogging around Òdena is defined by its rolling hills, rural paths, and views of historical sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogging routes to more challenging running trails. The routes in Òdena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1300 runners have used komoot to explore Òdena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Òdena?
A: There are over 10 dedicated running routes around Òdena, offering a variety of options for different fitness levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these trails over 1300 times.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes for beginners in Òdena?
A: Yes, Òdena offers several easy jogging routes suitable for beginners. You can find approximately 3 routes classified as easy, providing a gentle introduction to running in the area's scenic landscape.

Q: What are some challenging running trails for experienced runners in Òdena?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the region features routes like the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1115730427/path-of-fuente-de-ca-l-enric-and-ermita-de-san-bernabe-circular-from-odena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path of Fuente de Ca l'Enric and Ermita de San Bernabé — circular from Òdena&lt;/a&gt;. This 17.6 km trail includes significant elevation changes, passing by historical landmarks and natural springs.

Q: Are there circular running routes in Òdena?
A: Many of the running routes around Òdena are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17553689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell d'Òdena loop from Òdena&lt;/a&gt;, which offers views of the historic castle and surrounding countryside.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the jogging routes near Òdena?
A: The jogging routes around Òdena traverse a diverse landscape of rolling hills, agricultural fields, and scattered woodlands. You'll often encounter rural paths, historical sites, and natural springs, providing varied and picturesque views.

Q: What landmarks or points of interest can I see while running in Òdena?
A: Several routes pass by interesting landmarks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17553689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell d'Òdena loop from Òdena&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the historic Castell d'Òdena. You might also encounter natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3966366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roca Foradada (Holed Rock)&lt;/a&gt; or various mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4013542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Can Maçana&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Òdena?
A: The running trails in Òdena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 40 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the mix of rural paths and historical sights, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any running routes that start near Igualada?
A: Yes, there are routes accessible from Igualada. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10475714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Igualada&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.8 km trail that explores varied terrain near the town. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33811908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biollac loop from Igualada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find longer distance running trails in the Òdena area?
A: Absolutely. For longer distances, consider routes like the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1115730427/path-of-fuente-de-ca-l-enric-and-ermita-de-san-bernabe-circular-from-odena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path of Fuente de Ca l'Enric and Ermita de San Bernabé — circular from Òdena&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 17.6 km and takes over 2 hours to complete, offering a substantial workout.

Q: Are there any hidden gem running trails in Òdena?
A: While specific 'hidden gems' are often discovered by local exploration, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20064838" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barraca de Cal Barrab loop from Sesoliveres - la Massa&lt;/a&gt; offer a moderate 9.9 km experience through less frequented areas, providing a sense of discovery amidst the region's natural beauty.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Òdena?
A: Òdena's climate, characterized by rolling hills and open fields, generally makes spring and autumn ideal for running, with milder temperatures. Summer mornings and evenings can also be pleasant, while winters are typically mild enough for year-round activity, though some paths might be muddy after rain.
tips: Old castle that is located in the upper part of the town and whose tower is the best known element of the castle.
Source that is located near the stream that bears the same name and is also a good stop to take water and soak!
Immense farmhouse that is located in the upper parts of the town of Òdena and that allows us to see impressive views of the basin as a whole and, in the distance, of Montserrat.
The chapel of San Bernabé is located in Raval de l'Aguilera, close to what is called the Can Ferran farmhouse. The environment is beautiful close to the center of Òdena.
